response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), strong TypeScript across many years of product work, and current hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure including MCP code generators and agentic workflows — directly matching the AI-native baseline. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the resume, the deepest distributed-systems work was in DLang and ended in 2020, and there is little evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, or operational/on-call ownership at scale.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
Strong, long-standing TypeScript/Node.js expertise with craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python listed anywhere — a stated must-have skill for the role
Limited evidence of staff-scope responsibilities: technical strategy at scale, stakeholder management, or on-call/operational ownership
Most intensive distributed-systems experience is in DLang and dates to 2018–2020; recent roles skew full-stack product work

key_factors.factor: AI-agent/MCP tooling experience in current role
